channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Set-Jetting Travel Trend: Planning a Trip to Northern Ireland
item.description: The article explores the growing phenomenon of 'set-jetting,' where travelers visit locations featured in popular media, such as the fictional Winterfell from *Game of Thrones*. It highlights Northern...

item.title: Sarah Storey announces retirement at 48: Reflecting on a legendary Paralympic career
item.description: Sarah Storey, Britain's most decorated Paralympian with 74 medals across swimming and cycling, has announced her retirement from elite competition at the age of 48. The article details her remarkable ...

item.title: Alexander Zverev advances to Wimbledon semi-finals after defeating Taylor Fritz
item.description: German tennis star Alexander Zverev secured his first Wimbledon semi-final berth by defeating American Taylor Fritz in a three-set match on Wednesday. The second-seeded player, who had previously stru...

item.title: Technical access issues for News Corp Australia website users
item.description: News Corp Australia has implemented software to manage crawler bot traffic on its websites. If users are blocked and not using a crawler bot, they should temporarily disable AdBlockers or pop-up block...

item.title: Conservative Pundit Scott Jennings Fact-Checked Over Claims Trump Lowered Gas Prices
item.description: During a CNN panel discussion, conservative pundit Scott Jennings faced immediate fact-checking after asserting that gas prices are lower under President Trump compared to when he took office. Democra...

item.title: Megan Young Shares Emotional Moment as Son Leon Takes First Steps
item.description: Megan Young, the Filipino actress and former Miss Universe Philippines, recently shared a heartfelt moment as her son Leon took his first steps. The event, which marked a significant milestone in Leon...

item.title: The mental health impact of extreme photo editing in youth
item.description: Yashee and Mikala, hosts of the Two Blunt podcast, discuss their experiences with extreme photo editing on social media during their younger years. The episode explores how the pressure to present a f...

item.title: Ancient wisdom on finding happiness in the digital age
item.description: The article explores how ancient philosopher Plutarch's insights on balancing life and digital interactions remain relevant today. It highlights his advice on avoiding social media pitfalls, cultivati...

item.title: US judge orders Trump to pay $5 million to E. Jean Carroll in sexual abuse case
item.description: A US federal judge has ordered former President Donald Trump to pay E. Jean Carroll $5.8 million, including $5 million in damages and interest, after a 2023 civil trial found him liable for sexually a...

item.title: Critics Question Claims That Transformer Models Mimic Human Consciousness
item.description: The discussion revolves around a paper titled 'Transformer-Circuits' that claims its architecture resembles human consciousness. Critics, including users like liqu1d and phantomfive, argue the paper o...

item.title: WestJet flight attendants prepare for strike vote and planned work action
item.description: The article reports that WestJet flight attendants in Calgary are set to vote on a strike mandate, which would authorize a work stoppage if negotiations with the airline fail. The vote, which marks th...

item.title: Kenya's Affordable Housing Market Sees Surge in New Developers
item.description: Kenya's real estate market is undergoing a significant shift as demand for affordable housing grows, prompting new developers to enter the sector. Companies like Wilstone Homes are capitalizing on thi...

item.title: Building Collapse in Delhi Results in One Fatality and Multiple Injuries
item.description: A four-storey under-construction building collapsed in Rohini, Delhi, on Wednesday afternoon, resulting in the death of a 42-year-old motorcyclist, Ram Kishore, and the rescue of at least three worker...

item.title: Katie Price documentary lacks depth despite candid revelations
item.description: The article reviews the documentary 'Katie Price: Nothing to Hide,' critiquing its lack of substantive insight despite Price's candid disclosures. The piece explores Price's career trajectory from 199...

item.title: AgriSA's stance on exports and food security amid SAHRC inquiry
item.description: The South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC) is scrutinizing AgriSA's role in the country's food system, focusing on issues like food affordability, land reform, and farm worker welfare. During a...

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ and ‘Hacks’ Lead 2026 Emmy Nominations in Television Industry
item.description: The 2026 Emmy nominations have been announced, with the emergency room drama ‘The Pitt’ leading with 25 nods, while the final season of comedy ‘Hacks’ secured 24 nominations, a record for a comedy in ...

item.title: Pakistan and Russia Collaborate on Counterterrorism Strategy Against Afghan Networks
item.description: Pakistan and Russia have agreed to develop a joint strategy to combat terrorist networks operating in Afghanistan and the broader region. Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi and his Russian counterpart Vla...

item.title: Kenyan Church Leaders Launch Film Production Company to Boost Cinema Industry
item.description: Reverend Kathy Kiuna and Pastor Esther Obasi-Ike, two prominent Kenyan church leaders, have revealed plans to launch a film production company called Live Stage Production. The duo shared their ambiti...

item.title: Market Volatility Persists as Tech and Energy Sectors Show Mixed Performance
item.description: The stock market experienced mixed performance on July 8, 2026, with key sectors showing divergent trends. Micron Technology (MU) saw gains amid optimism about semiconductor demand, while Chevron (CVX...

item.title: Public outrage and state impunity in custodial deaths
item.description: The article examines how selective public outrage over custodial deaths often fails to hold the state accountable, reinforcing systemic impunity. It contrasts two cases: the 2020 deaths of Jeyaraj and...
